metadata.title: Messenginfo – USCIS Help for Ukrainians in the U.S.
metadata.description: Official-source USCIS guidance for Ukrainians: U4U re-parole, TPS, EAD, I-94, case status, and document translation. 4 languages. Not a law firm.
metadata.keywords: USCIS help, Ukraine immigration, re-parole U4U, TPS Ukraine, EAD work permit, I-94, case status checker, immigration self-help

hero.title: USCIS help for Ukrainians in the U.S.
hero.subtitle: Official sources, document guidance, and clear next steps for U4U, re-parole, TPS, EAD, I-94, and case tracking.

items: We are not licensed attorneys or immigration lawyers.
We provide information support, document translation assistance, and form-preparation help only.
For legal advice, please contact a licensed immigration attorney.

caseStatus.title: Check Your USCIS Case Status
caseStatus.description: Enter your receipt number to open the official USCIS case status page. Your number is never stored.
caseStatus.errorInvalid: Please enter a valid receipt number (e.g. IOE1234567890 or EAC1234567890).
caseStatus.privacyNote: Your receipt number is validated locally and never sent to our servers.

uscis-case-status.name: USCIS Case Status
uscis-case-status.description: Check the official status of your pending USCIS application.

uscis-forms.name: USCIS Forms
uscis-forms.description: Download official immigration forms and instructions from USCIS.

cbp-i94.name: CBP I-94
cbp-i94.description: View or print your official I-94 arrival/departure record.

doj-accredited.name: DOJ Accredited Representatives
doj-accredited.description: Find recognized organizations and DOJ-accredited immigration representatives.

services.title: What do you need help with?
services.subtitle: Select a topic to find official guidance, document checklists, and next steps.

parole-expires-soon.shortProblem: Understand your options before your humanitarian parole expires.

re-parole-u4u.shortProblem: Guidance on applying for re-parole under the U4U program.

tps-ukraine.title: TPS for Ukraine
tps-ukraine.shortProblem: Temporary Protected Status for Ukrainian nationals currently in the U.S.

ead-work-permit.title: EAD Work Permit
ead-work-permit.shortProblem: Apply for or renew your Employment Authorization Document (Form I-765).

i-94.title: I-94 Arrival/Departure Record
i-94.shortProblem: Access, print, or correct your I-94 record via the CBP portal.

uscis-case-status.title: USCIS Case Status
uscis-case-status.shortProblem: Track the status of a pending USCIS application using your receipt number.

payment-problem.title: Payment Problem
payment-problem.shortProblem: Resolve filing fee issues, rejected checks, or payment errors with USCIS.

biometrics.title: Biometrics Appointment
biometrics.shortProblem: Understand what to bring, what to expect, and how to reschedule biometrics.

rfe-denial.title: RFE or Denial
rfe-denial.shortProblem: Learn how to respond to a Request for Evidence or an application denial.

translate-document.title: Translate Document
translate-document.shortProblem: Upload a passport, birth certificate, or other document and prepare an English translation draft.

form-draft-helper.title: Form Draft Helper
form-draft-helper.shortProblem: Guidance on completing common USCIS forms correctly.

official-sources.title: Official Sources Library
official-sources.shortProblem: Curated links to USCIS, CBP, DOJ, and other authoritative government sources.

parole-expires-soon.title: My Parole Is Expiring Soon
parole-expires-soon.subtitle: Steps to take before your humanitarian parole expires.
whatHelps: Check your parole end date on your I-94 record at i94.cbp.dhs.gov
Review your eligibility for re-parole under the Uniting for Ukraine program
Explore whether Temporary Protected Status (TPS) applies to your situation
commonMistakes: Waiting until the last few days before expiration to take action
Confusing parole expiration with work authorization expiration
Failing to verify the correct end date on the I-94 record
parole-expires-soon.officialNote: USCIS provides official information about humanitarian parole at uscis.gov.

re-parole-u4u.title: Re-parole Under Uniting for Ukraine (U4U)
re-parole-u4u.subtitle: Official guidance on applying for re-parole through the U4U program.
whatHelps: Review the official USCIS Uniting for Ukraine program page for current requirements
Confirm your current parole status and expiration date via your I-94 record
Prepare required supporting documents as listed in official USCIS instructions
commonMistakes: Submitting the application without verifying current program requirements
Providing incomplete or inconsistent personal information
Misunderstanding the difference between parole and other immigration statuses
re-parole-u4u.officialNote: All re-parole information comes from the official USCIS Uniting for Ukraine page.

tps-ukraine.title: Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Ukraine
tps-ukraine.subtitle: What TPS means for Ukrainian nationals currently in the United States.
whatHelps: Visit the official USCIS TPS Ukraine page for current designation period and requirements
Understand TPS benefits: protection from removal and work authorization eligibility
Review the registration period and required forms on USCIS.gov
commonMistakes: Applying after the registration deadline without checking for late initial registration rules
Assuming TPS automatically renews without filing a re-registration application
Not maintaining continuous residence requirements
tps-ukraine.officialNote: TPS designation and requirements are published on the official USCIS TPS Ukraine page.

ead-work-permit.title: Employment Authorization Document (EAD) — Form I-765
ead-work-permit.subtitle: Guidance on applying for or renewing your EAD work permit.
whatHelps: Download Form I-765 and instructions from the official USCIS forms page
Identify the correct eligibility category for your immigration status
File before your current EAD expires to request an automatic extension
commonMistakes: Selecting the wrong eligibility category on Form I-765
Filing too late to take advantage of the automatic 180-day extension
Submitting without the correct supporting documents for your category
ead-work-permit.officialNote: Form I-765 and current filing fees are available at uscis.gov/i-765.

i-94.title: I-94 Arrival/Departure Record
i-94.subtitle: How to access, print, and understand your official I-94 record.
whatHelps: Access your I-94 record at i94.cbp.dhs.gov using your travel document information
Verify that the class of admission and date are correct
Print or save your most recent I-94 for USCIS or employer verification
commonMistakes: Assuming your visa expiration date equals your authorized stay — check the I-94
Not correcting errors on the I-94 promptly through the CBP deferred inspection process
Using an outdated I-94 when a newer one exists after re-entry
i-94.officialNote: I-94 records are maintained by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at i94.cbp.dhs.gov.

uscis-case-status.title: USCIS Case Status
uscis-case-status.subtitle: How to check the status of a pending USCIS application.
whatHelps: Use your receipt number (e.g. IOE1234567890) to check status at egov.uscis.gov
Sign up for case status updates by email on your USCIS online account
Understand common status messages and what they mean for your case
commonMistakes: Contacting USCIS before the published processing time has elapsed
Confusing the notice date with the receipt date
Not keeping the receipt notice in a safe place
uscis-case-status.officialNote: USCIS case status is available at the official USCIS case status portal egov.uscis.gov.

payment-problem.title: Filing Fee Payment Problems
payment-problem.subtitle: How to resolve payment issues with USCIS filings.
whatHelps: Review your payment method options on the official USCIS fee page at uscis.gov
Use the my.uscis.gov online account to manage payments for eligible forms
Contact USCIS through official channels if a fee was rejected or debited incorrectly
commonMistakes: Submitting incorrect fee amounts — always verify current fees before filing
Using an expired or unsupported payment method
Not including all required fees when filing multiple forms together
payment-problem.officialNote: Current USCIS filing fees are published at uscis.gov/forms/filing-fees.

biometrics.title: Biometrics Appointment
biometrics.subtitle: What to expect at your USCIS biometrics appointment.
whatHelps: Bring your biometrics appointment notice and a valid government-issued photo ID
Arrive on time — most appointments take 30 minutes or less
Contact USCIS in advance if you need to reschedule your appointment
commonMistakes: Forgetting to bring the biometrics appointment notice
Appearing without a valid photo ID
Missing the appointment without rescheduling, which can delay processing
biometrics.officialNote: Biometrics requirements are described on the official USCIS biometrics fee page.

rfe-denial.title: Request for Evidence (RFE) or Denial
rfe-denial.subtitle: Understanding and responding to an RFE or application denial.
whatHelps: Read the RFE or denial notice carefully — it specifies what evidence or information is needed
Respond within the deadline stated in the notice
Consult an accredited representative or licensed immigration attorney for complex RFEs
commonMistakes: Missing the response deadline, which can result in the application being denied
Providing incomplete responses that do not address all points in the RFE
Not consulting a professional when the RFE involves legal questions
rfe-denial.officialNote: USCIS Policy Manual guidance on RFEs is available at uscis.gov/policy-manual.

translate-document.title: Document Translation for USCIS
translate-document.subtitle: What USCIS requires when submitting foreign-language documents.
whatHelps: USCIS generally requires a full English translation and a translator certification statement
The translator must certify that they are competent to translate and that the translation is accurate
Review 8 CFR 103.2 for the official translation certification requirement
commonMistakes: Submitting a partial translation instead of a full translation
Omitting the translator certification statement
Using machine translation without human review and a proper certification
translate-document.officialNote: Translation requirements are codified at 8 CFR 103.2 (ecfr.gov).

form-draft-helper.title: USCIS Form Draft Helper
form-draft-helper.subtitle: Guidance on completing common USCIS forms correctly.
whatHelps: Always download the current version of the form from uscis.gov/forms
Read the official form instructions before filling out any field
Use black ink or complete the form electronically if allowed
commonMistakes: Using an outdated form version — USCIS may reject older versions
Leaving required fields blank or writing 'N/A' where not instructed
Not signing and dating the form before submission
form-draft-helper.officialNote: Current USCIS forms and instructions are available at uscis.gov/forms.

official-sources.title: Official Sources Library
official-sources.subtitle: Curated links to authoritative U.S. government immigration resources.
whatHelps: Use USCIS.gov as your primary source for immigration forms, fees, and policy
Verify attorney credentials at the DOJ accredited representatives roster
Check your I-94 arrival record at i94.cbp.dhs.gov
commonMistakes: Relying on unofficial websites or social media for immigration guidance
Using outdated information — always check the date of any resource you find
Paying third parties to access free government forms or information
official-sources.officialNote: All sources listed are official U.S. government websites.

labels.translationNotice: USCIS generally requires a full English translation with translator certification. This page creates a draft for review only.

translatePage.safeStatement: USCIS generally requires a full English translation with translator certification. This page creates a draft for review only.

askQuestion.title: Don't see your question?
askQuestion.subtitle: Browse our knowledge base or contact us.

howWeHelp.title: How We Help
howWeHelp.subtitle: Messenginfo connects you with official sources and organizes the information you need.

items.title: Find official sources
items.description: Every page links directly to USCIS.gov, CBP, DOJ, and other authoritative government sources.

items.title: Organize your documents
items.description: Understand what documents are needed for each immigration process and how to prepare them.

items.title: Get clear next steps
items.description: Step-by-step guidance on what to do next, based on official instructions.

documentTools.title: Document Translation Guidance
documentTools.subtitle: Understand USCIS requirements for translating foreign-language documents. Select a document type to learn what is needed.

passport.title: Passport
passport.description: Bio page translation requirements for USCIS submissions.

birth-certificate.title: Birth Certificate
birth-certificate.description: Full translation and certification requirements for birth records.

marriage-certificate.title: Marriage Certificate
marriage-certificate.description: What USCIS requires when submitting foreign marriage documents.

diploma.title: Diploma / Degree
diploma.description: Academic credential translation requirements for immigration purposes.

military-document.title: Military Document
military-document.description: Translation guidance for military service records and discharge papers.

driver-license.title: Driver's License
driver-license.description: When and how a driver's license must be translated for USCIS.

documentTools.footnote: Document tools will be added after verification. These pages explain what to prepare and link to official requirements.

telegramStrip.title: Community access
telegramStrip.fallbackText: Telegram channel and bot will be added after verification.

miaWidget.greeting: Hi! I'm Mia, your guide to Messenginfo.
miaWidget.disclaimer: I'm a quick navigator, not a chat assistant. For questions, contact us.

disclaimer.title: Messenginfo is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.
disclaimer.body: We provide official-source information and self-help guidance. For legal advice, consult a licensed immigration attorney or DOJ-accredited representative.

about.title: About
about.tagline: Official-source immigration information for Ukrainians in the U.S. Not a law firm.

404.title: Page not found
404.body: The page you are looking for does not exist.

500.title: Something went wrong
500.body: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.alert: Messenginfo is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.
disclaimer.notice: Pending legal review by a licensed attorney.

sections.title: Not a Law Firm
sections.body: Messenginfo is not a law firm, does not employ attorneys, and does not provide legal advice. Nothing on this website constitutes legal advice or creates an attorney-client relationship.

sections.title: Not Affiliated with the U.S. Government
sections.body: Messenginfo is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to USCIS, DHS, DOS, or any other U.S. government agency.

sections.title: For Legal Advice, Consult a Licensed Attorney
sections.body: For legal advice about your specific immigration situation, consult a licensed immigration attorney. The DOJ maintains a roster of recognized organizations and accredited representatives at justice.gov/eoir.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.notice: Pending legal review by a licensed attorney.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.body: By accessing or using messenginfo.com, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service.

sections.title: 2. About Messenginfo
sections.body: Messenginfo is an information service, not a law firm. We do not provide legal advice. Use of our service does not create an attorney-client relationship.

sections.title: 3. Disclaimers
sections.body: THE SITE IS PROVIDED AS-IS WITHOUT WARRANTIES. WE DO NOT PROVIDE LEGAL ADVICE. ALWAYS VERIFY INFORMATION AGAINST OFFICIAL SOURCES (USCIS.GOV).

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.notice: Pending legal review by a licensed attorney.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.body: Messenginfo operates messenginfo.com. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information.

sections.title: 2. Information We Collect
sections.body: We collect only what you provide via the contact form (name, email, message) and anonymized analytics via Vercel Analytics. We do not store receipt numbers or case details.

sections.title: 3. Your Rights
sections.body: You may request access, correction, or deletion of your data by contacting contact@messenginfo.com.

translationService.title: Translate Document
translationService.subtitle: Create a clean English translation draft from your original document. Use it to review names, dates, document fields, and prepare for a certified translator if needed.
translationService.draftOnlyBanner: Draft only — translator certification is not included in this step.
translationService.chooseDocumentSubtitle: Select the document type you want to prepare.

passport.title: Passport
passport.description: Upload a passport bio page and prepare a clear English draft.
fieldsIncluded: English draft with the main passport fields.
Name, date of birth, passport number, issue date, and expiration date.
Notes for visible stamps or seals when they affect the main page.
Draft PDF layout for review before translator certification.
uploadInstructions: Upload a clear photo or scan of the passport bio page.
Make sure all text is readable from edge to edge.
Avoid glare, blur, shadows, and cropped corners.
passport.riskNote: Review spelling, dates, and document numbers before final translator review.

birth-certificate.title: Birth Certificate
birth-certificate.description: Prepare a draft with names, dates, places, and registry details.
fieldsIncluded: Child and parent names as shown on the original.
Date and place of birth, certificate number, and registry office details.
Visible seals, notes, or marginal entries when readable.
Draft layout you can review before sending to a translator.
uploadInstructions: Upload the full certificate, front and back if text appears on both sides.
Keep stamps, seals, and handwritten notes visible.
Use a scan or flat photo with good contrast.
birth-certificate.riskNote: Birth records often include marginal notes. Keep the full page visible.

marriage-certificate.title: Marriage Certificate
marriage-certificate.description: Prepare a draft with spouses, dates, places, and registry details.
fieldsIncluded: Spouse names, date of marriage, and place of registration.
Certificate numbers, registry references, and issuing authority details.
Visible seals or annotations when they appear on the document.
Draft layout for quick review before translator certification.
uploadInstructions: Upload the full certificate, including all printed notes.
Do not crop off the registry area or seals.
Use a scan or high-resolution photo without glare.
marriage-certificate.riskNote: Marriage certificates often fail when the registry section is cropped out.

divorce-certificate.title: Divorce Certificate
divorce-certificate.description: Prepare a draft with parties, dates, decision details, and registry data.
fieldsIncluded: Names of both parties and the registration or decision date.
Court or registry references visible on the document.
Certificate numbers, issuing office, and official notes.
Draft layout for review before final translation steps.
uploadInstructions: Upload the complete document, including any court or registry pages.
Keep signatures, stamps, and handwritten notes visible when present.
Use clear lighting and avoid folded edges.
divorce-certificate.riskNote: If the divorce document has multiple pages, include all of them.

diploma-transcript.title: Diploma / Transcript
diploma-transcript.description: Prepare a draft with school names, dates, grades, and degree details.
fieldsIncluded: Institution name, degree or diploma title, and graduation dates.
Subjects, grades, or transcript rows when readable.
Issuing authority details and document numbers if shown.
Draft layout for reviewing names, dates, and academic terms.
uploadInstructions: Upload the diploma or transcript pages in full.
Keep tables, grades, and stamps readable.
Use PDF when the document has multiple pages.
diploma-transcript.riskNote: Academic records often need every page, not just the cover.

military-document.title: Military Document
military-document.description: Prepare a draft with service dates, unit details, and official notes.
fieldsIncluded: Service dates, unit references, and personal identification fields.
Visible orders, discharge notes, or classification details.
Stamps, seals, and handwritten notes when readable.
Draft format for review before translator certification.
uploadInstructions: Upload the full document, including all stamped pages.
Make sure handwriting is as clear as possible.
Use multiple photos or a PDF if the record spans several pages.
military-document.riskNote: Military records often contain handwritten notes that need manual review later.

driver-license.title: Driver License
driver-license.description: Prepare a draft with identity fields, dates, categories, and restrictions.
fieldsIncluded: Name, document number, date of birth, issue date, and expiration date.
Vehicle categories and visible restrictions or endorsements.
Issuing authority details when shown.
Draft format for checking core identity fields before final review.
uploadInstructions: Upload the front side and the back side if text appears on both sides.
Keep the card edges visible and avoid flash glare.
Use a flat photo or scan with readable small print.
driver-license.riskNote: If the back side contains codes or restrictions, upload it too.

other-document.title: Other Document
other-document.description: Prepare a draft for documents that do not fit the main categories.
fieldsIncluded: Core names, dates, document numbers, and issuing authority details.
Visible seals, signatures, and short notes where readable.
Simple draft layout for review before human translation steps.
A clear list of fields that may still need manual completion.
uploadInstructions: Upload the clearest complete copy you have.
Include every page that contains official text, seals, or signatures.
Use PDF if the document has more than one page.
other-document.riskNote: Other documents vary a lot. Review the draft carefully before using it.

upload.dropText: Drop your file here or click to upload
upload.privacyNote: Your file is used only to create the draft. Do not upload sensitive documents unless you agree to the privacy terms.
upload.localOnlyNotice: Draft generation is not active yet. This test screen shows the intended flow. No file is uploaded.
upload.fileTooLarge: This file is larger than 10 MB. Please upload a smaller file.
upload.unsupportedType: Unsupported file type. Use JPG, PNG, PDF, or HEIC.
upload.heicWarning: HEIC files may need conversion before draft generation is activated.

result.noBackend: Your draft flow is ready for the next step. AI generation will be connected after verification.
result.draftOnly: Draft only — translator certification is not included.

source.title: Official source requirement
source.body: USCIS generally requires foreign-language documents to include a full English translation with translator certification. This tool creates a draft only.
